response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable polyglot engineer with a long track record across web, mobile, and embedded domains, but the resume contains no meaningful experience with the competencies this role is built around: experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature-flag or experiment-assignment systems, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, rollout-risk management, or growth measurement. The highest-scale distributed-systems work (Dunnhumby, millions of HTTP req/s) is relevant background but is framed around low-latency data integration, not instrumentation or experimentation, and it ended in 2020. There is also no evidence of cross-team technical leadership at the scope an IC6 Principal role demands, and current and past titles remain at Senior or Staff level.
strengths: High-throughput distributed microservices experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O) demonstrates the ability to operate at relevant engineering scale.
Broad polyglot background covering TypeScript, C, C++, Java, and DLang, plus recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work, partially aligns with the team's AI-first engineering ethos.
Consistent software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source test tooling) across multiple roles signals strong engineering fundamentals.
concerns: No experimentation-platform experience of any kind: A/B testing systems, feature flagging, experiment assignment, bias detection, and rollout-risk management are entirely absent from the resume.

No telemetry or metrics infrastructure ownership; the role's primary deliverables—instrumentation foundations, unified growth measurement, and cross-surface funnels—have no analog in the candidate's history.
Seniority gap: titles have peaked at Senior/Staff with no evidence of driving multi-workstream architectural initiatives or cross-team influence at the Principal/IC6 scope the role requires.

key_factors.factor: No experimentation or feature-flag platform experience
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure ownership
